summary: This is a light personal post where O'Neil shares a moment of stress relief during final book edits by enjoying music with her three sons. She highlights songs by Rob Cantor as family favorites and mentions wanting to see him perform live. The post culminates in sharing the 'O'Neil/de Jong family anthem' - a Songify This video. It's a rare purely personal glimpse into her family life, stepping away from her usual algorithmic critique.
key_points: O'Neil uses family music time as stress relief during intense book editing
Rob Cantor is a family favorite, with each son having their preferred song
The family has an unofficial anthem they regularly sing together
The post shows O'Neil's self-deprecating humor, calling herself 'an emo'
money_quotes: I'm working through final edits today, and it's terribly stressful, so I'm glad I spent last night with my three sons listening to their favorite music.
Here's my fave Cantor tune (obviously, because I'm an emo)
We *make up* reasons to sing this song, and it can fairly be called the O'Neil/de Jong family anthem.
key_insight: Even data scientists immersed in algorithmic critique need moments of pure family joy and silly songs to balance the stress of serious work.
